title: chrysanthemum zawadzkii subsp. zawadzkii
intro: Chrysanthemum zawadzkii subsp. zawadzkii, also known as the Korean chrysanthemum or Pyeongwon, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family, native to Korea and China. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, headaches, and inflammation. Chrysanthemum zawadzkii subsp. zawadzkii is valued for its health benefits, including reducing blood pressure, improving circulation, and acting as an antioxidant. Herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, are made from the flowers, leaves, and roots of the plant, and are often used to promote overall well-being and alleviate various health conditions.
